rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use implementation scopes for bindings when they are valid in that context
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by inserting annex blocks in appropriate scopes
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when inserting new annex blocks
Avoid creating new dependencies between components during annex insertion
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are grounded in the current model or explicitly declared
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referencing component ports
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables
